name: hermaphroditus (with aphrodite)
shortDescription: Hermaphroditus was a son of Hermes and Aphrodite, known for his androgynous beauty and eventual merging with the nymph Salmacis to become a being possessing both male and female characteristics.
description: Hermaphroditus was a figure in Greek mythology, the son of Hermes, the messenger god and god of commerce, and Aphrodite, the goddess of love and beauty. He was renowned for his exceptional beauty. The most famous myth surrounding Hermaphroditus tells of his encounter with the nymph Salmacis. While bathing in a spring, Salmacis became enamored with Hermaphroditus. When he rejected her advances, she prayed to the gods that they might never be parted. The gods granted her wish by fusing their bodies together into a single being possessing both male and female characteristics. This resulted in the creation of a new being, Hermaphroditus, who embodied both genders. Ovid's *Metamorphoses* provides the most complete account of this transformation. Hermaphroditus is often depicted in art as a beautiful, androgynous figure. The myth of Hermaphroditus explores themes of love, desire, transformation, and the blurring of gender boundaries, making him a complex and enduring figure in classical mythology. He represents the fusion of male and female principles and the potential for transformation and change. His story also served as an etiological myth explaining the supposed enervating effects of the spring of Salmacis.
